description: Court record. Dated: Middle decade of Ṭevet 1417 Seleucid, which is December 1105 CE. Location: Fustat. Describing a married woman—Mudallala aka Sitt Baghdād—depositing part of her jewelry as collateral for a debt incurred by her brother, written on the reverse of the woman’s ketubba (marriage contract), as clearly stated: “The court entered the deposition of these objects on the back of her ketubba.” (S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 3:113, 449; 4:430.) Signed by Yiṣḥaq b. Shemuʾel. T-S 13J2.14 and T-S 10J28.14 are related to the same case (Goitein at one point suggested that they were two copies of the same document). EMS
description_he: פרוטוקול בית דין, דצמבר 1105. אישה בשם מודללה מפקידה תכשיטים כבטחון לחוב אחיה. כתוב על גב כתובתה.
